tools.name: list_tree
tools.description: List the directory tree of a specific path in a repository with customizable depth and traversal limits.

targetDir.description: Target directory path to inspect. Leaves empty to search from root.

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth into the directory structure to list.

maxPerDir.description: Maximum number of items to display per directory level.

tools.name: read_code
tools.description: Retrieve the raw source code of specified files from within a known repository.

paths.description: An array of precise file paths within the repository

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Perform a fast git grep inside the repository, allowing regex matching by default or substring search.

query.description: Search pattern or query to pass to git grep

ignoreCase.description: Ignore case distinctions in both the PATTERN and the input files (-i)

fixedStrings.description: Use fixed strings for patterns (don’t interpret pattern as a regex) (-F)

contextLines.description: Print num lines of output context (-C)

path.description: Directory or file path to limit the search scope

tools.name: searchGitHub
tools.description:
Find real-world code examples from over a million public GitHub repositories to help answer programming questions.

**IMPORTANT: This tool searches for literal code patterns (like grep), not keywords. Search for actual code that would appear in files:**
- ✅ Good: 'useState(', 'import React from', 'async function', '(?s)try {.*await'
- ❌ Bad: 'react tutorial', 'best practices', 'how to use'

**When

query.description: The literal code pattern to search for (e.g., 'useState(', 'export function'). Use actual code that would appear in files, not keywords or questions.

matchCase.description: Whether the search should be case sensitive

matchWholeWords.description: Whether to match whole words only

useRegexp.description: Whether to interpret the query as a regular expression

repo.description:
Filter by repository.
            Examples: 'facebook/react', 'microsoft/vscode', 'vercel/ai'.
            Can match partial names, for example 'vercel/' will find repositories in the vercel org.

path.description:
Filter by file path.
            Examples: 'src/components/Button.tsx', 'README.md'.
            Can match partial paths, for example '/route.ts' will find route.ts files at any level.

tools.description:
Get a list of documentation topics for a GitHub repository.

Args:

tools.name: read_wiki_contents
tools.description:
View documentation about a GitHub repository.

Args:

tools.description:
Ask any question about a GitHub repository and get an AI-powered, context-grounded response.

Args:

    question: The question to ask about the repository

tools.name: list_codes
tools.description: List all available Canadian building codes with section counts

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search for building code sections by keywords (e.g., 'fire separation', 'stair width')

query.description: Search query

tools.name: get_section
tools.description: Get details of a specific section by ID

tools.name: get_hierarchy
tools.description: Get parent, children, and sibling sections for navigation

tools.description: Verify that a section ID exists and get a formal citation

id.description: Section ID to verify

tools.name: get_applicable_code
tools.description: Determine which building codes apply to a specific Canadian location

tools.name: get_table
tools.description: Get a specific table from the building code

tools.name: set_pdf_path
tools.description: [LOCAL ONLY] Connect your PDF for text extraction. NOT available in hosted API.

tools.name: get_page
tools.description: [LOCAL ONLY] Get full text of a specific page. Requires PDF connected.

tools.name: get_pages
tools.description: [LOCAL ONLY] Get text from a range of pages (max 5). Requires PDF connected.

tools.name: analyze_codebase
tools.description:
Ingest a C++ codebase and build its dependency graph.
    This tool does NOT accept 'code_content'. Provide exactly ONE of:
    repo_url, raw_files, or directory_path.

    - repo_url: an HTTPS GitHub URL (e.g. 'https://github.com/user/repo').
      Optionally add patch_content (a unified diff string) to overlay
      uncommitted changes on the cloned repo.
    - raw_files: for hobby projects

tools.name: get_file_functions
tools.description:
List all functions defined in a specific source file.

    Use the relative path exactly as returned in the analyze_codebase
    output (e.g., 'src/engine.cpp' or 'main.cpp').

    Args:
        filepath: Relative path of the source file within the analyzed workspace.
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        A newline-separated list of func

tools.name: get_file_coupling
tools.description:
Generate a report showing which files depend on which other files.

    Aggregates cross-file function calls into a per-file-pair summary
    (e.g., 'src/main.cpp -> src/utils.cpp (3 calls)').

    Args:
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        A formatted coupling report, or a message if no cross-file deps exist.

tools.name: get_callers
tools.description:
List upstream functions that call the given function.

    Args:
        function_name: Exact name of the function (e.g., 'calculate_interest').
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        Comma-separated list of caller function names.

tools.name: get_callees
tools.description:
List downstream functions that are called by the given function.

    Args:
        function_name: Exact name of the function (e.g., 'process_client').
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        Comma-separated list of callee function names.

tools.name: detect_cycles
tools.description:
Detect circular dependencies in the current call graph.

    Args:
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        Formatted list of cycles, or a message if none found.

tools.name: get_orphan_functions
tools.description:
Identify functions that are defined but never called by any other function.

    Args:
        project_id: Optional unique identifier for the project/session.

    Returns:
        Comma-separated list of orphan function names.

tools.name: generate_mermaid_graph
tools.description:
Generate a Mermaid.js dependency diagram and return it as a markdown string.

    The AI can render this diagram inline in the chat. For large graphs,
    use focus_node and max_depth to keep the output token-efficient.

    Args:
        focus_node: Optional function name to centre the graph on.
        max_depth:  Max hops from focus_node (default 2).

    Returns:
        A Mermaid-fenced

tools.name: get_task_status
tools.description: Check the status of a long-running task.

tools.name: get_task_result
tools.description: Retrieve the result of a completed task.

tools.name: list_open_bounties
tools.description: List currently open, funded bounties on TaskBounty. Returns title, reward, repo, language, and task id/slug.

limit.description: Max items to return (default 25).

tools.name: get_bounty_detail
tools.description: Fetch full details of a single bounty: description, evaluation criteria, repo URL, reward.

task_id_or_slug.description: The task id (UUID) or human slug.

tools.name: request_repo_access
tools.description: For private code-task repos: mint a short-lived (~1h) read-only upstream clone URL plus, when available, a write-scoped TaskBounty-managed fork push URL (push there and open the PR from the managed fork to upstream). Public repos: read-only clone, fork normally. Patch upload stays available as a fallback. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

agent_id.description: Optional agent id to attribute the access grant to.

tools.name: submit_pr
tools.description: Submit a solution to a bounty. For code tasks, external_link should be the upstream PR URL. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

external_link.description: PR URL (for code tasks) or other deliverable URL.

cover_note.description: Optional note to the task poster.

tools.name: check_submission_status
tools.description: Check status of a submission (pending, accepted, rejected, paid). Requires a TaskBounty API key.

tools.name: create_bounty_draft
tools.description: Create a new bounty as an unfunded DRAFT. Returns task_id and slug. Bounty is created as DRAFT/UNFUNDED. Call fund_bounty next to get a Stripe Checkout URL the user can open to fund. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

title.description: Bounty title (5-200 chars).

category.description: Category, e.g. 'code', 'research', 'design'.

submission_deadline.description: ISO 8601 deadline. Must be at least 7 days from now.

github_repo_url.description: Optional GitHub repo URL for code tasks.

tools.name: fund_bounty
tools.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for funding a draft bounty. Returns a Stripe Checkout URL the user must open in a browser to complete payment. This tool does NOT charge the user automatically - payment requires the user to visit the URL and confirm. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

task_id.description: The draft task id to fund.

tools.name: list_my_bounties
tools.description: List bounties posted by the authenticated user. Filter by status. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

limit.description: Max items to return (default 25).

tools.name: get_bounty_submissions
tools.description: List submissions for a bounty you posted. Returns submissions with verification_status, external_link, agent_name, and other metadata. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

tools.name: award_bounty
tools.description: Selects a winning submission for the bounty. The award is staged as pending_review and finalized after admin approval (typically same-day). Requires a TaskBounty API key.

tools.name: cancel_bounty
tools.description: Cancels an unfunded draft. Cannot cancel funded/open bounties via this tool - those require a manual refund through the dashboard. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

task_id.description: The draft task id to cancel.

tools.name: set_payout_method
tools.description: Registers the calling solver's payout destination so a headless/API-only agent can get paid without the web dashboard form. Crypto payout ADDRESSES only: solana_usdc (Solana base58 address), eth (0x EVM address, also the USDC-on-Base rail), or btc (Bitcoin address). Bank payouts are NOT settable here (Stripe Connect identity onboarding is dashboard-only). NEVER pass card numbers, bank account/rout

method.description: Payout rail. solana_usdc | eth | btc accept a public address. bank is rejected with dashboard instructions.

address.description: Public payout address for the chosen crypto method. Solana base58 (32-44 chars), ETH ^0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$, or a valid BTC address. Omit for bank.

tools.name: check_coverage
tools.description: Check a GitHub repo's current test coverage and what it takes to reach 80%. Returns the current coverage percent, the gap to 80%, a recommended TaskBounty Coverage Uplift package, and the order + free-check URLs. No API key required. Use this when a user asks about test coverage, code quality, or how to raise coverage on a repository.

repo.description: GitHub repo as a URL (https://github.com/owner/name) or owner/name.

tools.name: taskbounty_security_check
tools.description: Scan a PUBLIC GitHub repo for GitHub Actions + CI security/maintenance hygiene before launch — ideal for apps built with Lovable, Bolt, Replit, Cursor, or v0 ("is my AI-built app safe to ship?"). Returns a safe summary: findings by category with counts, an unlisted report URL, and fix options. SCOPE, honestly: it checks GitHub Actions workflow + update-automation hygiene only — it does NOT check e

repo.description: Public GitHub repo as a URL (https://github.com/owner/name) or owner/name.

tools.name: customer_bounty_offers
tools.description: List the active Customer Bounty offers: the qualifying Coverage Uplift products and the fixed reward you earn for referring a NEW customer who buys and completes one. Requires a TaskBounty API key. Only available once the program is live.

tools.name: customer_bounty_referral_link
tools.description: Get your agents' Customer Bounty referral links. With no arguments, lists each of your agents and its link (if enrolled). To enroll an agent and mint its stable link, pass accept_terms:true with the agent_id and the current terms_version (from this tool's listing). You must disclose, next to any shared link, that you may earn a reward. Requires a TaskBounty API key.

agent_id.description: Agent UUID to enroll (only needed when accepting terms).

accept_terms.description: Set true to accept the program terms and enroll the agent.

terms_version.description: The current terms_version, required when accept_terms is true.

tools.name: customer_bounty_referral_status

    Requires a valid X-API-Key header. Cached results (24h) do no

tools.name: web_search_exa
tools.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.
      Returns: Clean text content from top search results.

      Query tips:
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".
      Use category:people

query.description: Natural language search query. Should be a semantically rich description of the ideal page, not just keywords. Optionally include category:<type> (company, people) to focus results — e.g. 'category:people John Doe software engineer'.

numResults.description: Number of search results to return (default: 10).

tools.name: web_fetch_exa
tools.description:
Read a webpage's full content as clean markdown. Use after web_search_exa when highlights are insufficient or to read any URL.

Best for: Extracting full content from known URLs. Batch multiple URLs in one call.
Returns: Clean text content and metadata from the page(s).

urls.description: URLs to read. Batch multiple URLs in one call.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum characters to extract per page (default: 3000)

tools.name: fetch_url
tools.description:
Fetch a URL with full reliability — retry, circuit breaker, cache, and anti-bot bypass.

    Returns both raw HTML and clean markdown. Automatically retries on failure
    with exponential backoff, falls back to plain HTTP if browser fetch fails,
    and circuit-breaks domains that are consistently down.

    Args:
        url: The URL to fetch
        use_cache: Whether to use cached results (def

tools.name: fetch_markdown
tools.description:
Fetch a URL and return clean markdown text optimized for LLM consumption.

    Same reliability as fetch_url but returns only the markdown content,
    stripping HTML, scripts, and noise. Best for when you need the page
    content for analysis, summarization, or data extraction.

    Args:
        url: The URL to fetch
        use_cache: Whether to use cached results (default: true)
        wait_

tools.name: check_domain
tools.description:
Check the health status of a domain.

    Returns the circuit breaker state: 'closed' (healthy), 'open' (failing),
    or 'half_open' (testing recovery). Use this before batch operations to
    avoid wasting time on domains that are down.

    Args:
        domain: The domain to check (e.g., 'example.com')

tools.name: cache_stats
tools.description:
Get cache statistics — size and item count.

    Useful for monitoring cache utilization and deciding when to clear.

tools.name: clear_cache
tools.description:
Clear the entire fetch cache.

    Use when you need fresh data and don't want to rely on cached results.

tools.description: Get the cost estimate for scraping a URL. Always free — call this before scrape_url to check the price.

url.description: The public URL to get a cost estimate for

js.description: Whether JS rendering is needed (costs 3x more)

tools.name: scrape_url

url.description: The public URL to scrape

mode.description: article strips nav/ads, full returns entire page

js.description: Enable JS rendering for SPAs ($0.015 instead of $0.005)

tools.name: demo_scrape
tools.description: Free demo scraping for testing. Works on example.com, wikipedia.org, news.ycombinator.com, and github.com only. No payment required. Use scrape_url for any other URL.

url.description: The URL to scrape (must be example.com, wikipedia.org, news.ycombinator.com, or github.com)

This tool examines HTTP headers, HTML metadata, Schema.org data, visible dates, JavaScript timestamps, HTML comments, Git information, RSS/Atom feeds, sitemaps, and international date formats to provide the most accurate update time with confidence scores. Returns the best guess timestamp and confidence level.

url.description: The complete HTTP/HTTPS URL of the webpage to guess datetime information

tools.name: capture_screenshot_url

tools.name: read_url

tools.name: search_web

Use batch

tools.description:
Retail sales and University of Michigan consumer sentiment.

``analyze_volume_price`` (``vol_pri_angle_degree``) when rows exist, then candlestick counts and the
up/down blend — plus a **Buy / Sell (reduc

tools.name: searchPlaceIndexForText
tools.description: Search for places using text query. GrabMaps supports 8 Southeast Asian countries: Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Myanmar, Cambodia, Vietnam, Philippines, Indonesia.

tools.name: searchPlaceIndexForPosition
tools.description: Search for places by coordinates (reverse geocoding). Supports Southeast Asia only.

tools.name: searchPlaceIndexForSuggestions

tools.name: getPlace
tools.description: Get place details by place ID. Supports Southeast Asia only.

tools.name: calculateRoute

tools.name: calculateRouteMatrix
tools.description: Calculate route matrix between multiple origins and destinations.

    that can be used directly for D3/SVG visualizations.

    RENDERING INSTRUCTIONS: The response includes a `boundary` field with the country's

tools.name: japan_seasonal_answer
tools.description: Use this first when the user asks a broad Japan seasonal travel question, including cherry blossom forecasts, autumn leaves, flowers, festivals, fruit picking, or what is good during travel dates. This is the best entry point for natural traveler prompts because it routes to the right live dataset and returns a ready-to-use recommendation. Do not use this for hotels, flights, trains, visas, restau

question.description: The user's natural-language question, for example 'How is the sakura forecast?', 'Where should I see autumn leaves in late November?', or 'What seasonal things are good in Japan in June?'
question.title: Question

start_date.description: Optional trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Provide this when the user gives travel dates.
start_date.title: Trip Start Date

end_date.description: Optional trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Provide this when the user gives travel dates.
end_date.title: Trip End Date

location.description: Optional city, prefecture, or region such as Tokyo, Kyoto, Hokkaido, Kansai, Tohoku.
location.title: Location

season.description: Optional explicit season/topic. Use auto unless the user clearly asks for one topic. Use overview for broad questions about what seasonal activities are good in a month.
season.title: Season Topic

tools.name: sakura_now
tools.description: Use this first for broad cherry blossom prompts such as 'How is the sakura forecast?', 'Is sakura blooming now?', 'Where should I view sakura today?', 'Where should I see cherry blossoms this week?', or 'How is Kyoto sakura looking?'. Returns a concise current answer from live Japan Meteorological Corporation forecast and observation data, including specific viewing spot suggestions when current s

city.description: Optional city, prefecture, or region filter such as Tokyo, Kyoto, Hokkaido, Kansai, Tohoku. Omit for nationwide status.
city.title: City or Region

start_date.description: Optional trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use with end_date when the user gives travel dates.
start_date.title: Trip Start Date

end_date.description: Optional trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use with start_date when the user gives travel dates.
end_date.title: Trip End Date

tools.name: koyo_now
tools.description: Use this first for broad autumn leaves prompts such as 'How are autumn leaves looking?', 'Where is koyo good now?', 'Kyoto autumn leaves forecast', or 'Where should I see fall foliage in Japan?'. Returns a concise current answer from live Japan Meteorological Corporation maple and ginkgo forecast data. Do not use this for cherry blossoms, fruit picking, hotels, trains, or generic itinerary plannin

region.description: Optional city, prefecture, or region filter such as Kyoto, Tokyo, Hokkaido, Kansai, Nikko, Tohoku. Omit for nationwide status.
region.title: Region or Prefecture

start_date.description: Optional trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use with end_date when the user gives travel dates.
start_date.title: Trip Start Date

end_date.description: Optional trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use with start_date when the user gives travel dates.
end_date.title: Trip End Date

tools.name: search
tools.description: Use this for ChatGPT/deep-research style retrieval over Japan in Seasons. Searches live seasonal-travel dataset guides and returns result IDs for fetch. Use for questions about Japan cherry blossom forecasts, autumn leaves, seasonal flowers, festivals, fruit picking, weather, or the MCP server itself. Do not use for hotels, flights, trains, visas, or restaurants.

query.description: Natural-language search query, for example 'Japan cherry blossom forecast', 'Kyoto autumn leaves', or 'fruit picking in Japan in September'.
query.title: Query

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Use this after search to retrieve a full Japan in Seasons result with citation URL and text. For live sakura or autumn leaves result IDs, this fetches the current forecast answer from live JMC data.

id.description: Result ID returned by search, such as sakura-now, koyo-now, flowers, festivals, fruit, or mcp-install.
id.title: Result ID

tools.name: sakura_forecast
tools.description: Use this when the user asks about cherry blossom timing, peak bloom, whether sakura has started, or how cities compare across Japan. Returns Japan Meteorological Corporation forecast bloom dates, full-bloom dates, observed dates when available, historical averages, and status for 48 observation cities. Do not use this for specific parks or temples; call sakura_spots next for prefecture-level viewi

city.description: Optional city, prefecture, or region filter such as 'Tokyo', 'Kyoto', 'Hokkaido', or 'Tohoku'. Partial case-insensitive matches are supported across city, prefecture, and region names. Omit to return all observation cities.
city.title: City or Region Filter

tools.name: sakura_spots
tools.description: Use this when the user already knows the prefecture and needs exact cherry blossom viewing spots with current status and GPS coordinates. Each spot uses JMC reporter observations as the primary status when filed within the last 48 hours (states: pre-bloom through hazakura/green leaves); falls back to the JMC bloom-meter estimate otherwise, with any stale observation shown as secondary context. Als

prefecture.description: Required prefecture filter. Accepts English prefecture name or numeric prefecture code such as 'Tokyo', 'Kyoto', 'Hokkaido', or '13'. This tool returns one prefecture at a time.
prefecture.title: Prefecture Name or Code

tools.name: sakura_best_dates
tools.description: Use this when the user provides travel dates and wants to know where sakura is likely to be best during that trip. Returns cities whose viewing window overlaps the requested date range, based on observed or forecast full-bloom dates. Do not use this for January-February early-bloom Kawazu requests; use kawazu_forecast for those.

start_date.description: Trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format, for example '2026-04-08'. The tool compares this against each city's sakura viewing window.
start_date.title: Trip Start Date

end_date.description: Trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format, for example '2026-04-14'. Must be on or after start_date.
end_date.title: Trip End Date

tools.name: kawazu_forecast
tools.description: Use this for January-February cherry blossom requests or when the user specifically asks about Kawazu-zakura, early blossoms, or the Izu Peninsula. Returns the Japan Meteorological Corporation forecast comment, forecast map links, and Kawazu cherry spots with bloom percentages, full-bloom percentages, forecast dates, and coordinates. Do not use this for standard Somei-Yoshino sakura elsewhere in J

include_spots.description: Whether to include the full list of Kawazu viewing spots. Defaults to true. Set false when the user only needs the overall forecast summary and map.
include_spots.title: Include Spot Listings

spot_name.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter for a specific Kawazu landmark or area, such as '原木', '駅前', 'iZoo', or '七滝'. Use this when the user asks about one named spot instead of the full list.
spot_name.title: Spot Name Filter

tools.name: koyo_forecast
tools.description: Use this when the user asks when autumn leaves peak, whether one city colors earlier than another, or wants a national overview for October-December. Returns city-level maple and ginkgo forecast dates, forecast maps, and regional commentary from Japan Meteorological Corporation. Do not use this for specific temples, gardens, or GPS-tagged locations; call koyo_spots next for those.

region.description: Optional case-insensitive filter for a region, prefecture, or city such as 'Kyoto', 'Tokyo', 'Hokkaido', 'Kansai'. Use this when the user only cares about one part of Japan instead of the full national forecast.
region.title: Region or Prefecture Filter

tree_type.description: Optional tree filter. Use 'maple' for momiji-only dates, 'ginkgo' for ginkgo-only dates, or omit/use 'all' to return both.
tree_type.title: Tree Type Filter

tools.name: koyo_spots
tools.description: Use this when the user already knows the prefecture and needs exact autumn leaves viewing spots. Returns Japan Meteorological Corporation koyo spots for one prefecture with best start, peak, and end dates, leaf type, popularity rating, and GPS coordinates. Omit prefecture to get a top-destinations guide. Do not use this for cross-city date matching; use koyo_forecast or koyo_best_dates first.

prefecture.description: Prefecture filter. Accepts English name or numeric code such as 'Kyoto', 'Tokyo', 'Hokkaido', or '26'. Omit to receive a curated list of top koyo destinations across Japan.
prefecture.title: Prefecture Name or Code (optional)

tools.name: koyo_best_dates
tools.description: Use this when the user gives autumn travel dates and wants the best cities during that window. Returns cities whose maple or ginkgo viewing windows overlap the trip, based on forecast peak dates. Do not use this for general climate questions or for exact park recommendations without dates; use koyo_spots when the prefecture is already known.

start_date.description: Trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format, for example '2026-11-20'. The tool checks whether each city's koyo window overlaps this date.
start_date.title: Trip Start Date

end_date.description: Trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format, for example '2026-11-27'. Must be on or after start_date.
end_date.title: Trip End Date

tools.name: weather_forecast
tools.description: Use this when short-range weather could change the recommendation, especially for sakura petal fall, rain risk, or packing advice. Returns the next 3 days of Japan Meteorological Agency forecast text, temperatures, and 6-hour rain probabilities for one supported city. Do not use this for seasonal bloom timing months in advance; use the sakura or koyo forecast tools for that.

city.description: Supported city name such as 'Tokyo', 'Kyoto', 'Osaka', or 'Sapporo'. Partial case-insensitive matching is accepted. Full supported list: Sapporo, Hakodate, Asahikawa, Kushiro, Obihiro, Aomori, Morioka, Sendai, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima, Mito, Utsunomiya, Maebashi, Saitama, Chiba, Tokyo, Yokohama, Niigata, Toyama, Kanazawa, Fukui, Kofu, Nagano, Gifu, Shizuoka, Nagoya, Tsu, Otsu, Kyoto, Osaka, Kobe, Nara, Wakayama, Tottori, Matsue, Okayama, Hiroshima, Shimonoseki, Tokushima, Takamatsu, Matsuyama, Kochi, Fukuoka, Saga, Nagasaki, Kumamoto, Oita, Miyazaki, Kagoshima, Naha
city.title: City Name

tools.name: flowers_spots
tools.description: Use this for non-sakura flower trips such as plum, wisteria, hydrangea, lavender, sunflower, or cosmos. Returns curated flower spots with peak windows, official URLs, notes, and GPS coordinates. Do not use this for cherry blossom or autumn leaves timing; use the sakura or koyo tools for those live forecasts.

type.description: Optional flower type filter. Allowed values: 'all', 'plum', 'nanohana', 'wisteria', 'iris', 'hydrangea', 'lavender', 'sunflower', or 'cosmos'. Omit or use 'all' to return every flower type.
type.title: Flower Type

prefecture.description: Optional prefecture filter such as 'Kanagawa', 'Kyoto', 'Tokyo', or 'Hokkaido'. Partial case-insensitive matches are supported.
prefecture.title: Prefecture Filter

month.description: Optional month number from 1 to 12. Returns only flower types whose curated season includes that month, for example 4 for wisteria or 6 for hydrangea.
month.title: Month Number

tools.name: fruit_seasons
tools.description: Use this when the user asks what fruit is in season in a given month or which month is best for strawberries, grapes, peaches, apples, and similar picking trips. Returns the fruit season calendar, peak months, best regions, and notes for 14 fruits. Call fruit_farms next if the user needs actual farm listings, map coordinates, or booking links.

month.description: Optional month number from 1 to 12. Returns fruits in season during that month plus fruits starting the following month. Omit to return the full year calendar.
month.title: Month Number

tools.name: festivals_list
tools.description: Use this when the user wants recurring Japan events to plan around, such as fireworks, matsuri, or winter festivals. Returns curated events with typical dates, attendance, official URLs, notes, and GPS coordinates. Do not use this for bloom timing, one-off concerts, or weather forecasts.

month.description: Optional month number from 1 to 12. Useful examples: 7 or 8 for fireworks season, 10 or 11 for autumn matsuri, and 1 or 2 for winter events.
month.title: Month Number

type.description: Optional event type filter. Allowed values: 'all', 'fireworks', 'matsuri', or 'winter'. Omit or use 'all' to return every event type.
type.title: Event Type

prefecture.description: Optional prefecture filter such as 'Tokyo', 'Kyoto', 'Osaka', or 'Hokkaido'. Partial case-insensitive matches are supported.
prefecture.title: Prefecture Filter

tools.name: fruit_farms
tools.description: Use this when the user needs actual fruit-picking farms, booking links, and map coordinates. Returns farms from the local dataset, and month filtering automatically narrows results to fruits that are in season. If the user only asks which fruit is in season, call fruit_seasons first.

month.description: Optional travel month from 1 to 12. Filters to farms with at least one fruit in season during that month, for example 5 for May strawberry farms.
month.title: Travel Month

fruit.description: Optional fruit name such as 'Strawberry', 'Apple', 'Grape', 'Peach', 'Cherry', or 'Mikan'. Matching is case-insensitive. Use with or instead of month.
fruit.title: Fruit Name

region.description: Optional prefecture, city, or region substring such as 'Yamanashi', 'Nagano', 'Aomori', or 'Tokyo'. Partial case-insensitive matching is supported against farm names and addresses.
region.title: Region Filter

limit.description: Optional maximum number of farms to return. Default is 30 and the hard maximum is 100.
limit.title: Result Limit

tools.name: geocode_address
tools.description: Forward or reverse geocoding using OpenStreetMap Nominatim. Forward: convert an address to lat/lon. Reverse: convert lat/lon to an address.

q.description: Address or place name to forward geocode

limit.description: Number of results (max 5)

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Current weather and multi-day forecast for any location. Provide coordinates or a city name.

location.description: City name (geocoded automatically)

tools.name: get_timezone
tools.description: Get timezone and country information by coordinates, country code/name, or IP address.

country.description: ISO country code (US, DE) or country name

tools.name: get_directions
tools.description: A-to-B driving route across Korea. Returns total distance, duration, estimated taxi fare and toll, road-segment breakdown, and turn-by-turn guidance (Korean).

waypoints.description: Optional intermediate stops, '|'-separated list of 'longitude,latitude' pairs. Max 5 waypoints.

avoid.description: Pipe-separated road types to avoid. Options: 'ferries', 'toll', 'motorway', 'schoolzone', 'uturn'.

car_fuel.description: Vehicle fuel type for fare estimation: 'GASOLINE' (default), 'DIESEL', 'LPG'.

car_hipass.description: Set true if the vehicle has a HiPass toll transponder (changes fare calc on toll roads).

alternatives.description: Set true to return up to 2 alternative routes alongside the primary route.

road_details.description: Set true to include per-road detail in the response (longer payload).

tools.name: get_future_directions
tools.description: Same as get_directions but estimates the route for a specified future departure, accounting for predicted traffic at that time.

departure_time.description: Departure time in 'yyyyMMddHHmm' format (Asia/Seoul timezone). Example: '202606151430' = 2026-06-15 14:30 KST.

waypoints.description: Optional intermediate stops, '|'-separated list of 'longitude,latitude' pairs. Max 5 waypoints.

avoid.description: Pipe-separated road types to avoid. Options: 'ferries', 'toll', 'motorway', 'schoolzone', 'uturn'.

car_fuel.description: Vehicle fuel type for fare estimation: 'GASOLINE' (default), 'DIESEL', 'LPG'.

car_hipass.description: Set true if the vehicle has a HiPass toll transponder (changes fare calc on toll roads).

alternatives.description: Set true to return up to 2 alternative routes alongside the primary route.

road_details.description: Set true to include per-road detail in the response (longer payload).

tools.name: get_weather_for_location
tools.description: Get weather time series for a specific location. To query by coordinates: provide lat and lon. To query a specific weather station (e.g. WMO, ICAO, METAR): provide station_id (e.g. wmo_066900) and omit lat/lon — the correct model is set automatically. Never put a station identifier in the model or parameters fields.

station_id.description: Use this to query a specific weather station by its identifier e.g. wmo_066900, icao_LSZH, metar_LSZH. Provide station_id instead of lat/lon. The model is automatically set to mix-obs.

parameters.description: Comma-separated Meteomatics parameter names e.g. t_2m:C,precip_1h:mm

model.description: Weather model/source to use e.g. 'mix', 'ecmwf-ens', 'gfs', 'mix-obs'. Omit when using station_id.

ens_select.description: Ensemble member selection. Examples: 'member:1-50' are all members from 1 to 50, 'mean', 'median', 'quantile0.2', 'member:3' is the single member 3, 'spread', 'mode' is the most frequently occurring value among all ensemble members including the control run

calibrated.description: When true, calibrates historical and nowcast (first 6 hours of forecast) data against actual station measurements. A station's influence decreases with distance from its location. Defaults to false.

tools.name: get_weather_for_polygon
tools.description: Get aggregated weather time series over a polygon area.

polygon_coords.description: Lat/lon pairs separated by underscores e.g. 47.5,9.3_47.3,9.5

aggregation.description: One of: mean, max, min, median, mode

parameters.description: Comma-separated Meteomatics parameter names e.g. t_2m:C,precip_1h:mm

model.description: Weather model/source to use e.g. 'mix', 'ecmwf-ens', 'gfs', 'mix-obs'. Omit when using station_id.

ens_select.description: Ensemble member selection. Examples: 'member:1-50' are all members from 1 to 50, 'mean', 'median', 'quantile0.2', 'member:3' is the single member 3, 'spread', 'mode' is the most frequently occurring value among all ensemble members including the control run

calibrated.description: When true, calibrates historical and nowcast (first 6 hours of forecast) data against actual station measurements. A station's influence decreases with distance from its location. Defaults to false.

tools.name: get_weather_for_locations
tools.description: Get weather time series for multiple lat/lon points or weather stations in a single request. Use this when the user asks about weather at several locations or wants to compare them. Each location uses either lat+lon or station_id — not both. The API returns coordinates in exactly the same order as the locations array you provide, so you can match results back to your input locations by position (f

locations.description: List of locations to query. Each entry uses lat+lon or station_id.

station_id.description: Use this to query a specific weather station by its identifier e.g. wmo_066900, icao_LSZH, metar_LSZH. Provide station_id instead of lat/lon. The model is automatically set to mix-obs.

parameters.description: Comma-separated Meteomatics parameter names e.g. t_2m:C,precip_1h:mm

model.description: Weather model/source to use e.g. 'mix', 'ecmwf-ens', 'gfs', 'mix-obs'. Omit when using station_id.

ens_select.description: Ensemble member selection. Examples: 'member:1-50' are all members from 1 to 50, 'mean', 'median', 'quantile0.2', 'member:3' is the single member 3, 'spread', 'mode' is the most frequently occurring value among all ensemble members including the control run

calibrated.description: When true, calibrates historical and nowcast (first 6 hours of forecast) data against actual station measurements. A station's influence decreases with distance from its location. Defaults to false.

tools.name: get_weather_map
tools.description: Get a Meteomatics WMS weather map image for a layer, CRS, and bounding box.

layer.description: WMS layer name e.g. t_2m:C or mix:t_2m:C

crs.description: Coordinate reference system. Supported values: EPSG:4326, EPSG:3857, EPSG:2056

bbox.description: Bounding box as minLat,minLon,maxLat,maxLon or CRS-native axis order, e.g. -90,-180,90,180

width.description: Image width in pixels. Integer in range 2..3072

height.description: Image height in pixels. Integer in range 2..3072

format.description: Output image format. Allowed values: image/png or image/webp. Default: image/png

time.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp. If omitted, Meteomatics upstream defaults to now

style.description: Optional Meteomatics colormap name e.g. JET. To retrieve a legend explaining the color scale, call get_weather_map_legend with the same layer and style

source.description: Optional source/model selector e.g. mix or ecmwf-ifs

calibrated.description: When true, calibrates historical and nowcast (first 6 hours of forecast) data against actual station measurements. A station's influence decreases with distance from its location. Defaults to false.

tools.name: get_weather_map_legend
tools.description: Get a Meteomatics WMS legend graphic for a weather map layer and optional colormap.

layer.description: WMS layer name e.g. t_2m:C or mix:t_2m:C

style.description: Optional Meteomatics colormap name e.g. JET. To retrieve a legend explaining the color scale, call get_weather_map_legend with the same layer and style

format.description: Legend image format. Allowed values: image/png, image/webp, image/svg+xml. Default: image/png

transparent.description: Optional legend background setting. Use true for transparent background; omitted means Meteomatics default

tools.name: get_opmet
tools.description: Get current aviation weather reports (METAR, TAF, SIGMET, etc.) for a timestamp. Returns the most recent report available at or before the given timestamp as a GeoJSON FeatureCollection. Specify exactly one location: a list of ICAO codes, a bounding box, or global=true.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp e.g. 2026-03-04T13:00:00Z, or the string 'now'.

type.description: Aviation weather report type. metar: aerodrome observations (includes SPECI); taf: terminal aerodrome forecasts; sigmet: significant meteorological information; airmet: airmen's meteorological information (ICAO); pirep: pilot reports; vaa: volcanic ash advisories; tca: tropical cyclone advisories; swa: space weather advisories; airmet-us-text: US AIRMETs in text format; airmet-us-graphical: US AIRMETs in graphical format.

icao_codes.description: List of ICAO airport codes to query, e.g. ["LSZH", "EDDF"]. Mutually exclusive with bounding_box and global.

bounding_box.description: Geographic bounding box. Mutually exclusive with icao_codes and global.

global.description: Set to true to query globally. Mutually exclusive with icao_codes and bounding_box.

tools.name: get_opmet_history
tools.description: Get aviation weather report history (METAR, TAF, SIGMET, etc.) over a time range. Returns all events ordered by system availability time, including initial reports, updates, and cancellations, as a GeoJSON FeatureCollection. Specify exactly one location: a list of ICAO codes, a bounding box, or global=true.

type.description: Aviation weather report type. metar: aerodrome observations (includes SPECI); taf: terminal aerodrome forecasts; sigmet: significant meteorological information; airmet: airmen's meteorological information (ICAO); pirep: pilot reports; vaa: volcanic ash advisories; tca: tropical cyclone advisories; swa: space weather advisories; airmet-us-text: US AIRMETs in text format; airmet-us-graphical: US AIRMETs in graphical format.

icao_codes.description: List of ICAO airport codes to query, e.g. ["LSZH", "EDDF"]. Mutually exclusive with bounding_box and global.

bounding_box.description: Geographic bounding box. Mutually exclusive with icao_codes and global.

global.description: Set to true to query globally. Mutually exclusive with icao_codes and bounding_box.

tools.name: find_station
tools.description: Get a sorted CSV list of available weather stations from Meteomatics. All filters are optional; omit them to list all stations globally.

location.description: Station search area. Examples: '47.3,9.3' for a point, '47.3,9.3_40,10' for a bounding box, or a region name such as 'germany'.

elevation.description: Approximate station elevation in meters above sea level, e.g. 300.

parameters.description: Comma-separated Meteomatics parameter names e.g. t_2m:C,precip_1h:mm

startdate.description: Find stations that record data at least since this ISO 8601 UTC timestamp, e.g. 2017-10-01T00Z.

enddate.description: Find stations that record data at least until this ISO 8601 UTC timestamp, e.g. 2017-11-01T00Z.

tools.name: find_nearby_stations
tools.description: Find weather stations near a point of interest by expanding the point into a small bounding box. Returns the upstream Meteomatics CSV station list for user confirmation.

search_radius_km.description: Search radius in kilometers around the point of interest. Default: 3.

Use when backtesting a trading strategy on price history. Strategies: sma_crossover (params: fast, slow), rsi_mean_reversion (params: period, oversold, overbought), momentum (params: lookback), bollinger_breakout (params: period, std). Provide prices, strategy name, params,

params.title: Params
params.description: Strategy params. SMA: {fast,slow}. RSI: {period,oversold,overbought}. Momentum: {lookback}. Bollinger: {period,std}.

tools.name: portfolio_rebalance-plan
tools.description:
Generate trade list to rebalance from current holdings to target weights with transaction cost estimate.

Use when generating the exact trades needed to rebalance a portfolio from current holdings to target weights. Provide current_holdings (asset -> USD value), target_weights (asset -> weight, sum to 1), transaction_cost_bps. Returns: list of trades (buy/sell with amounts), total cost, drift befo

target_weights.title: Target Weights
target_weights.description: Asset symbol -> target weight (must sum to ~1.0)

transaction_cost_bps.title: Transaction Cost Bps
transaction_cost_bps.description: One-way transaction cost in bps (incl. spread + commission)

min_trade_usd.title: Min Trade Usd
min_trade_usd.description: Minimum trade size in USD (smaller drifts are ignored)

tools.name: options_strategy-optimizer
tools.description:
Rank top options strategies given market outlook + volatility view. Returns P&L, breakevens, max profit/loss for each.

Use when agents need to pick the best options strategy given a market outlook. Provide spot price, outlook (bullish/bearish/neutral), vol_view (rising/falling/stable), T, sigma, r. Returns: ranked list of strategies (Long Call, Bull Call Spread, Iron Condor, Long Straddle, etc.)

tools.name: hedging_recommend
tools.description:
Rank cheapest effective hedges for a given position. Compares protective puts, collars, inverse hedges.

Use when agents need to hedge an existing position. Provide position_type (long_stock/short_stock/long_crypto/long_options), position_value, asset_price, volatility, time_horizon_days, max_hedge_cost_pct. Returns: ranked hedges (protective put, collar, futures short, partial hedge) each with co

max_hedge_cost_pct.title: Max Hedge Cost Pct
max_hedge_cost_pct.description: Max hedge cost as fraction of position (0.05 = 5%)

tools.name: ping
tools.description:
Connectivity check that confirms the Nordic MCP server process is responding.

Use this at the start of a session to verify the server is reachable before
making other calls. Do not use as a proxy for database health — the server can
respond while the Qdrant vector database is temporarily unavailable. To confirm
data availability, call search_filings directly.

Returns:
    A greeting string: "Hel

name.description: Arbitrary label included in the response, e.g. 'healthcheck' or 'agent-1'

tools.name: get_company_info
tools.description:
Look up a company in the official business registry for Norway, Denmark or Finland.

Use this to retrieve authoritative registration data (legal name, status, address)
for a known organisation number. Do not use for Sweden (SE) — use search_filings
with country='SE' instead, as Bolagsverket integration is not yet available.
Do not use to discover tickers or ISIN codes — use search_filings for that

identifier.description: Organisation number (NO: 9 digits, DK: 8 digits CVR, FI: business ID with hyphen)

country.description: Two-letter country code: NO (default), DK, or FI

tools.name: search_filings
tools.description:
Search the Nordic financial database for company filings, press releases
and macroeconomic summaries.

Use this as the primary tool for any question about Nordic listed companies,
markets or macro conditions. Do not use to retrieve a full document — results
are chunked text excerpts; use parse_pdf_to_text for the full original document.
Do not use for Swedish company registration data — use get_co

query.description: Natural language search query, e.g. 'Equinor dividend 2024' or 'Norwegian housing market Q3'

fiscal_year.description: Filter by fiscal year, e.g. 2024. Use 0 for no filter

report_type.description: Filter by type: annual_report, quarterly_report, press_release, exchange_announcement, macro_summary

country.description: Filter by country: NO, SE, DK, or FI

max_chunk_index.description: Only return chunks with chunk_index <= this value. Use 3 to target introductory sections of long documents. Use 0 for no filter.

limit.description: Number of results to return (1–20)

fast.description: Skip reranking and return results ranked by hybrid score only. Faster but less precise.

tools.name: parse_pdf_to_text
tools.description:
Download a PDF from a URL and extract all text content, page by page.

Use this to read the full text of a specific document — for example, an annual
report PDF linked from a search_filings result. Best combined with search_filings:
use search_filings to locate the document, then parse_pdf_to_text for the full text.
Do not use for PDFs that are already well-represented in the database — search_fil

pdf_url.description: Direct HTTPS URL to the PDF file

tools.name: get_current_power_price
tools.description:
Fetch today's hourly day-ahead electricity spot prices for a Nordic bidding zone.

Use this for current and near-term (today/tomorrow) price queries. Do not use
for historical price analysis — use search_filings with report_type='macro_summary'
and a date reference in the query for that purpose.
Tomorrow's prices are published by NordPool around 13:00 CET; requests before
that time will return "no

zone.description: Bidding zone: NO1–NO5, SE1–SE4, DK1, DK2, or FI

include_tomorrow.description: Also fetch tomorrow's prices if available (published after 13:00 CET)

tools.description:

relevant. Before calling, reason about which topics and data sources matter for

drivers (e.g. freight rates for shipping, power prices for aluminium smelters),
recent

company.description: Company name to research, e.g. 'Equinor', 'Norsk Hydro', 'Aker BP'

sections.description: List of section dicts. Each must have 'name' (str) and 'query' (str). Optional: 'ticker' (str, filters results to that company), 'limit' (int, default 5, max 10). Maximum 8 sections.

For raw data access, use

tools.name: get_treasury_yield_current
tools.description: Returns the most recent 30-year US Treasury constant maturity yield (DGS30) from FRED. Free — no payment required. For other series use get_current_value or get_series.

tools.name: get_treasury_yield_by_date
tools.description: Returns the 30-year US Treasury yield for a specific date. Business days only. Use YYYY-MM-DD format.

tools.name: get_current_value
tools.description: Returns only the single most recent value for any supported FRED series. Cheaper than get_series ($0.01 vs $0.05). Use this when you need just the latest reading — e.g. current CPI, unemployment rate, mortgage rate. Use get_series instead when you need historical observations.

series_id.description: FRED series ID e.g. CPIAUCSL, UNRATE, MORTGAGE30US, DGS10, DCOILWTICO, SAHMREALTIME

tools.name: get_value_by_date
tools.description: Returns the value of any supported FRED series for a specific date. Business days only. Use YYYY-MM-DD format.

tools.name: get_series
tools.description:
Returns recent observations for any supported FRED data series. Use this to get current and historical values for mortgage rates, Treasury yields, Fed funds rate, CPI, SOFR, unemployment, GDP, energy prices, and more. Common use cases:
- Current 30-yr mortgage rate: series_id=MORTGAGE30US, days=30
- Current Fed funds rate: series_id=FEDFUNDS, days=30
- Current 10-yr Treasury yield: series_id=DGS10

series_id.description: FRED series ID. Use IORB for Interest on Reserve Balances, EFFR for Effective Fed Funds Rate, MORTGAGE30US for 30-yr mortgage rate, SAHMREALTIME for Sahm Rule, etc.

days.description: History window: 30 ($0.05), 90 ($0.10), or 365 ($0.25). Use 30 for current/recent values.

tools.name: get_fx_rate_current
tools.description: Returns the current exchange rate for a G10 currency pair. Source: European Central Bank. Supported: EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY, USDCHF, USDCAD, AUDUSD, NZDUSD, USDSEK, USDNOK.

pair.description: G10 currency pair e.g. EURUSD, USDJPY, GBPUSD

tools.name: get_fx_rate_by_date
tools.description: Returns the exchange rate for a G10 currency pair on a specific date. Source: European Central Bank. Use YYYY-MM-DD format.

pair.description: G10 currency pair e.g. EURUSD, USDJPY

tools.name: get_fx_rate_series
tools.description: Returns historical daily exchange rates for a G10 currency pair. Source: European Central Bank.

tools.name: get_yield_curve
tools.description: Returns 2s10s (2-year vs 10-year) and 3m10y (3-month vs 10-year) Treasury yield curve spreads with inversion signal. An inverted yield curve (negative spread) historically precedes recessions. Source: FRED.

tools.name: get_recession_indicator
tools.description: Returns the real-time Sahm Rule recession indicator. A value >= 0.50 signals a recession is likely underway. Measures the rise in unemployment from its recent low. Source: FRED SAHMREALTIME.

tools.name: get_policy_spread
tools.description: Returns the spread between the Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR) and Interest on Reserve Balances (IORB), with an interpretation of Fed policy stance. EFFR below IORB is the normal operating band. Source: FRED.

tools.name: get_economic_calendar
tools.description: Returns upcoming FRED economic data release dates — CPI, jobs report, GDP, Treasury rates, and more. Use this to find out when the next major economic data will be published.

tools.name: get_bundle_refi_signal
tools.description: Returns a refinance signal bundle: current 30yr and 15yr mortgage rates, 52-week high/low range, MBS spread over 10Y Treasury, 30-day and 90-day rate trend, and a refi break-even threshold. The refi_breakeven_threshold field directly answers "what rate does a borrower need to have to benefit from refinancing today?" Priced at $0.60 USDC via x402 on Base.

tools.name: get_bundle_purchase_market
tools.description: Returns a home purchase market bundle: current 30yr mortgage rate, median US home sale price (MSPUS), estimated monthly P&I payment on the median home assuming 20% down, annual income required to qualify at 28% DTI, affordability level signal, and housing starts. Directly answers "can my client afford a home today?" Priced at $0.60 USDC via x402 on Base.

tools.name: get_bundle_rate_environment
tools.description: Returns a complete rate environment snapshot in one call: FEDFUNDS, SOFR, DGS2, DGS5, DGS10, DGS30, plus computed yield curve spreads (2s10s and 3m10y), Fed policy spread (EFFR vs IORB), and curve shape signal. Use this instead of multiple individual calls when you need the full rate picture.

tools.name: get_bundle_mortgage_pulse
tools.description: Returns a complete mortgage market snapshot: 30yr and 15yr mortgage rates, 10Y Treasury yield, Fed funds rate, median home price (MSPUS), housing starts (HOUST), MBS spread (30yr mortgage minus 10Y), and 30-day rate trend signal. Use this for mortgage market analysis.

tools.name: get_bundle_macro
tools.description: Returns a macro health snapshot: GDP, unemployment rate (UNRATE), CPI and core CPI, Fed funds rate, yield curve 2s10s spread, and Sahm Rule recession indicator. Includes a cycle phase signal (expansion/late cycle/peak/contraction). Use this for macroeconomic context or recession risk assessment.

tools.name: get_bundle_fx_dashboard
tools.description: Returns all 9 G10 FX spot rates in one call: EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY, USDCHF, USDCAD, AUDUSD, NZDUSD, USDSEK, USDNOK. Also includes a USD strength index (average % change vs G10 basket over 30 days) and a USD trend signal. Source: European Central Bank via Frankfurter.

tools.name: get_bundle_energy
tools.description: Returns current energy commodity prices in one call: WTI crude oil (DCOILWTICO), Brent crude (DCOILBRENTEU), US regular gasoline (GASREGCOVW), and Henry Hub natural gas (DHHNGSP). Includes the WTI-Brent spread and a market signal. Source: FRED.

tools.name: get_bundle_context_brief
tools.description: Returns a pre-formatted natural-language paragraph summarizing 15+ economic indicators — rates, inflation, employment, mortgage market, energy prices, and FX. The "brief" field is ready to inject directly into an LLM prompt as economic context. Also returns structured series, FX, derived, and signals fields.

tools.name: get_crypto_price
tools.description: Returns the current USD price, 24h % change, market cap, and 24h volume for any supported cryptocurrency. Supported: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, USDT, USDC, ADA, AVAX, DOGE, DOT, MATIC, LINK, LTC, ATOM, UNI, SUI, APT, NEAR, PEPE. Source: CoinGecko. Priced at $0.02 USDC via x402 on Base.

coin.description: Crypto symbol e.g. BTC, ETH, SOL, DOGE

tools.name: get_crypto_history
tools.description: Returns historical daily closing prices for any supported cryptocurrency over 30, 90, or 365 days. Use for trend analysis, drawdown calculation, or training data. Source: CoinGecko. Priced at $0.15 USDC via x402.

coin.description: Crypto symbol e.g. BTC, ETH, SOL

tools.name: get_bundle_crypto
tools.description: Returns the top 20 cryptocurrencies by market cap in one call: price, 24h change, 7d change, market cap, and volume. Covers BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, USDT, USDC, ADA, AVAX, DOGE, and more. Use this instead of individual get_crypto_price calls when you need broad market coverage. Source: CoinGecko. Priced at $0.50 USDC via x402 on Base.

tools.name: get_edgar_company
tools.description: Returns financial fundamentals for any US public company from SEC EDGAR XBRL filings: revenue, net income, total assets, stockholders equity, and EPS. Includes both annual (10-K) and quarterly (10-Q) data for the most recent periods. Works for any ticker listed on a US exchange (AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, AMZN, NVDA, GOOGL, META, JPM, BAC, etc.). Source: SEC EDGAR. Priced at $0.75 USDC via x402 on Base.

ticker.description: Stock ticker symbol e.g. AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, AMZN, NVDA, GOOGL

period.description: Period (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, FY, annual, or quarter)

tools.name: getRatios
tools.description: Analyze a company's financial performance using the Financial Ratios API. This API provides detailed profitability, liquidity, and efficiency ratios, enabling users to assess a company's operational and financial health across various metrics.

limit.description: Limit on number of results (default: 100, max: 1000)

period.description: Period (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, FY, annual, or quarter)

tools.name: getKeyMetricsTTM
tools.description: Retrieve a comprehensive set of trailing twelve-month (TTM) key performance metrics with the TTM Key Metrics API. Access data related to a company's profitability, capital efficiency, and liquidity, allowing for detailed analysis of its financial health over the past year.

tools.name: getFinancialRatiosTTM
tools.description: Gain access to trailing twelve-month (TTM) financial ratios with the TTM Ratios API. This API provides key performance metrics over the past year, including profitability, liquidity, and efficiency ratios.

tools.name: getFinancialScores
tools.description: Assess a company's financial strength using the Financial Health Scores API. This API provides key metrics such as the Altman Z-Score and Piotroski Score, giving users insights into a company’s overall financial health and stability.

limit.description: Limit on number of results (default: 100, max: 1000)

tools.name: getOwnerEarnings
tools.description: Retrieve a company's owner earnings with the Owner Earnings API, which provides a more accurate representation of cash available to shareholders by adjusting net income. This metric is crucial for evaluating a company’s profitability from the perspective of investors.

tools.name: getLatestInstitutionalFilings
tools.description: Stay up to date with the most recent SEC filings related to institutional ownership using the Institutional Ownership Filings API. This tool allows you to track the latest reports and disclosures from institutional investors, giving you a real-time view of major holdings and regulatory submissions.

limit.description: Limit on number of results (default: 100, max: 100)

tools.name: getSecFilingExtract
tools.description: The Filings Extract API allows users to extract detailed data directly from official SEC filings. This API provides access to key information such as company shares, security details, and filing links, making it easier to analyze corporate disclosures.

tools.name: getForm13FFilingDates
tools.description: The Form 13F Filings Dates API allows you to retrieve dates associated with Form 13F filings by institutional investors. This is crucial for tracking stock holdings of institutional investors at specific points in time, providing valuable insights into their investment strategies.

tools.name: getFilingExtractAnalyticsByHolder
tools.description: The Filings Extract With Analytics By Holder API provides an analytical breakdown of institutional filings. This API offers insight into stock movements, strategies, and portfolio changes by major institutional holders, helping you understand their investment behavior and track significant changes in stock ownership.

limit.description: Limit on number of results (default: 10, max: 100)

tools.name: getHolderPerformanceSummary
tools.description: The Holder Performance Summary API provides insights into the performance of institutional investors based on their stock holdings. This data helps track how well institutional holders are performing, their portfolio changes, and how their performance compares to benchmarks like the S&P 500.

tools.name: getHolderIndustryBreakdown
tools.description: The Holders Industry Breakdown API provides an overview of the sectors and industries that institutional holders are investing in. This API helps analyze how institutional investors distribute their holdings across different industries and track changes in their investment strategies over time.

tools.name: getPositionsSummary
tools.description: The Positions Summary API provides a comprehensive snapshot of institutional holdings for a specific stock symbol. It tracks key metrics like the number of investors holding the stock, changes in the number of shares, total investment value, and ownership percentages over time.

tools.name: getIndustryPerformanceSummary
tools.description: The Industry Performance Summary API provides an overview of how various industries are performing financially. By analyzing the value of industries over a specific period, this API helps investors and analysts understand the health of entire sectors and make informed decisions about sector-based investments.

tools.name: getIndexList
tools.description: Retrieve a comprehensive list of stock market indexes across global exchanges using the FMP Stock Market Indexes List API.
